func NewFenwickTree(size int) *FenwickTree {

        size: size,
        tree: make([]int, size+1),
    }

func (ft *FenwickTree) Update(index int, delta int) {
    for ; index <= ft.size; index += index & -index {
        ft.tree[index] += delta

    res := 0
    for ; index > 0; index -= index & -index {
        res += ft.tree[index]
    }
    return res
}

func (ft *FenwickTree) RangeQuery(l int, r int) int {
    return ft.Query(r) - ft.Query(l-1)
}

func solve(nums []int64, queries [][]int64) []int64 {
    n := len(nums)
    // Initialize Fenwick Trees for depths 0-4
    fts := make([]*FenwickTree, 5)
    for i := 0; i < 5; i++ {
        fts[i] = NewFenwickTree(n)
    }
    // Precompute depths for each number
    for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
        d := computeDepth(nums[i])
        fts[d].Update(i+1, 1)
    }
    // Process queries
    res := make([]int64, 0, len(queries))
    for _, q := range queries {
        if q[0] == 1 {
            // Type 1 query: [1, l, r, k]
            l := int(q[1])
            r := int(q[2])
            k := int(q[3])
            // Check if k is within 0-4
            if k < 0 || k > 4 {
                res = append(res, 0)
                continue
            }
            // Query the Fenwick Tree for depth k in [l, r]
            count := fts[k].RangeQuery(l+1, r+1)
            res = append(res, int64(count))
        } else {
            // Type 2 query: [2, idx, val]
            idx := int(q[1])
            val := q[2]
            // Get old value
            oldVal := nums[idx]
            oldDepth := computeDepth(oldVal)
            // Update the Fenwick Tree for old depth
            fts[oldDepth].Update(idx+1, -1)
            // Update the value in nums
            nums[idx] = val
            // Compute new depth
            newDepth := computeDepth(val)
            // Update the Fenwick Tree for new depth
            fts[newDepth].Update(idx+1, 1)
        }
    }
